label: Squarish-shell 2-STOREY 2BR / 2 baths, REAR-CORNER STAIR, hall-less ground floor — open great room across the full front (entry, dining folds in with a counter on the kitchen edge), kitchen + T&B in a rear wet bay, straight stair in the rear corner boarding off the great room. Upstairs, the squarish signature: BOTH bedrooms side-by-side at the front with street-facing windows, landing hall behind them, second T&B at the rear near the wet stack. Canonical 10x10 lot (6x6 buildable per floor, 72 m² gross — inside the 2BR/2-bath 65-80 m² band).

adjacencies.note: GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.
GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightline
GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom end
GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service door
VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)
2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wall
2F: master door off the landing hall
2F: second bedroom door off the landing hall
2F: second T&B doors off the landing hall

soft_proximities.note: 2F: pull the upper T&B toward the rear stair corner — lands it near the GF wet bay (kitchen + bath1 share the rear band), shortening the vertical plumbing run. Soft only: cross-floor wet-stack alignment isn't a hard v2 constraint yet.

zone_split.note: 2F-only geometric partition (all listed rooms are storey 2, per the multi-storey zone_split rule): both bedrooms hold the FRONT band — the squarish signature, side-by-side street-facing windows — with the T&B strictly behind the split line. 'public_rooms' here is the mechanical 'other side of the split' list, not a zoning claim; hall2 and stairwell float.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: stair_run_geometry
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive — 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m. min_greatest_dim_m pins the run; max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight. The common_bath caps stop post-snap gap-filling from stretching a bath into a corridor (post-snap aspect is unchecked); 2.6 m / 3.9 m² is roomier than the narrow 2s sibling's 2.5 / 2.5 because this 36 m²-per-floor shell can afford proper baths.

notes: 2-STOREY squarish catalog topology #1 — REAR-CORNER STAIR, hall-less GF
(authored 2026-07-19 per MULTISTOREY_V2_DESIGN.md and the
multistorey-topology-authoring checklist; first 2s topology outside the
narrow shell).
Why this parti is the SQUARISH take on a 2-storey 2BR: the ~6 m buildable
width fits TWO bedrooms side-by-side across the front — both get
street-facing windows — which the 4 m narrow shell can never do (its 2s
pair stacks master front / br2 rear instead). The extra width also feeds
a proper great room (~6 m wide) and roomier baths than the narrow
sibling. Canonical 10x10 lot = 6x6 buildable x 2 floors = 72 m² gross,
landing INSIDE the locked 2BR/2-bath 65-80 m² floor band that the
narrow 2s pair (48-64 m²) undershoots.
Canonical layout (10 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 6 x 6 buildable per floor):
GF:  Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear edge)]
Rear:  [stair | T&B | kitchen              ]  kitchen rear-pinned
2F:  Front: [br2        | master               ]  side-by-side
Mid:   [stairwell | hall2 landing         ]
Rear:  [stairwell | T&B | master closet   ]
Stair: straight, 0.9-1.2 m wide, >= 3.5 m run, 15 risers @ 200 mm /
250 mm treads, left-anchored into the rear corner; boards off the great
room, arrives at hall2 (stair_boarding / stair_arrival hold the two
mouths to opposite ends of the run).
Structure/override choices (each per an established convention):
- zone_split is a 2F-ONLY horizontal partition pinning both bedrooms
to the front band with bath2 behind — the side-by-side-bedrooms
signature, stated as one split line instead of anchor lists (the
3BR catalog's over-constrained-anchors trap; only the stair is
anchored, single-member list, the proven-safe pattern).
- zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as
the private wing — same reasoning as the narrow hall-less sibling:
bedrooms-alone can't outweigh a full-width GF great room + kitchen.
- Hall-less GF: at 36 m²/floor the great room is the circulation
(stair boards off it, bath1 doors into it) per the compact
convention; a _hall sibling for larger squarish lots (with
fallback_below_buildable_sqm routing back here) is future work.
- soft proximity bath2->stairwell pulls the upper bath toward the
rear corner, near the GF wet bay, to shorten the vertical plumbing
run (cross-floor wet-stack alignment is still soft-only in v2).
Naming note: the `bath` token undercounts the program (two common
baths, one per floor) — kept consistent with the 2s narrow siblings
pending the 2s naming-convention pass.
